action_text: Open the chart options menu for “All Incidents by Assignment”
rationale: Opening the chart’s options may provide “Show data” or similar to view a sortable table of counts by assignee, making it easy to find the minimum and the corresponding agents.

action_text: Hover over a nearby small bar in the chart to see its count tooltip
rationale: We need to identify the minimum count; hovering adjacent small bars will reveal tooltips with counts to find agents with the lowest value.

action_text: Hover over another small bar slightly to the left to view its tooltip
rationale: Continue scanning small bars to find any with the minimum incident count (likely 1); this hover reveals another agent’s count.

action_text: Hover over an additional small bar to display its incident count
rationale: Further checks of adjacent bars help confirm the true minimum across agents before proceeding to create problems.

action_text: Hover over a small bar toward the right end of the chart to read its count
rationale: The far-right small bars may represent the lowest counts; hovering there can quickly reveal if any agents have the minimum (e.g., 1).
